title: Adriano Belisario
text:
Adriano Belisario combines expertise in communication, open-source technologies and data to address important social concerns. He currently collaborates with the Joint Data Center on Forced Displacement and the Netlab laboratory from the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro. Belisario holds an MSc in Social Data Science from the Oxford Internet Institute (University of Oxford) and collaborated with organizations like the Open Knowledge Brazil, Witness, Forensic Architecture, UNESCO, UNHCR, and Agência Pública.


This website showcases my previous work and research on a variety of topics such as data journalism & digital methods & open-source technologies & social data science & human rights & open-source intelligence (OSINT) & investigation of crimes and corruption & data-driven investigations & machine learning & natural language processing & independent media & digital culture & community-based infra-structure & free spectrum & independent media and networks & cultural policies & free culture & more.









About me, my interests and experience.


BIO








Academic and journalistic articles, data investigations and other publications in English and Portuguese.


TEXT








Events I’ve organized and collaborated on.


EVENTS






This website is under development, and not all sections have completed information yet.
Built with Quarto inspired by Ella Kaye’s template.

title: Adriano Belisario
text:
EVENTS
Since 2010, I have organized events in Brazil and actively participated in multiple international events. This is a non-exhaustive list of activities I have developed, including conferences, lectures, workshops and courses.


Organization
I organized several editions of the Brazilian Conference on Data Journalism and Digital Methods (Coda.Br) from 2018 to 2022, including the first regional meeting in the Amazon region. Previously, I collaborated with Witness to organize the first national meeting on deepfakes and developed a series of events about free culture and piracy (Copyfight).

Courses
As head of the School of Data Brazil, I created, coordinated and taught several online and in-person trainings between 2019 and 2022, such as Dados 360, Jornalismo de dados ambientais: no rastro do desmatamento da Amazônia, Investigações digitais: OSINT para jornalistas e ativistas. title: Adriano Belisario
text:
Adriano Belisario combines expertise in communication and data to address important human rights concerns. As a long-time open-source advocate, he has combined advanced technological applications with journalistic investigations and social research to address issues like police violence and socio-environmental challenges.
Belisario is currently part of the Joint Data Center on Forced Displacement, a collaboration between the World Bank and UNHCR. Before this role, he spearheaded the School of Data program at Open Knowledge Brasil and contributed to organizations like Witness, Forensic Architecture UNESCO, and Agência Pública. His publication portfolio includes books, academic articles, and journalistic pieces in Portuguese and English, and he has been invited to speak at several global events, such as Re: Publica and RightsCon.
Belisario taught data journalism in Brazilian postgraduate courses and holds an MSc in Social Data Science from the Oxford Internet Institute (University of Oxford). While there, he collaborated with Instituto Fogo Cruzado to develop a language model that uses social media data to identify gun violence reports in real time. He also earned an MA in Communication and Culture from the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro (UFRJ). In partnership with Medialab/UFRJ and Agência Autônoma, Belisario co-developed Documental.xyz, an award-winning initiative that employs diverse investigative methods, such as Natural Language Processing, OSINT, and forensic architecture, with storytelling to equip grassroots communities with compelling evidence related to human rights violations.

Awards and recognitions
Bellingcat’s technical writing fellowship (2024)
Finalist in the category “Social Action” at the Study UK Alumni Award (2024)
Shortlisted at the Gabo Awards (2021)
Winner of the Jornalismo-Mosca Award (2021)
Ling Institute’s Visionary Journalist Scholarship (2020)


Education
Master of Science in Social Data Science (with distinction). Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford. 2022-2023.
Post-baccalaureate certification (with honors). Lede Program. Columbia University of the City of New York. 2021
Masters of Arts in Communication and Culture. Federal University of Rio de Janeiro. 2016.
Bachelor in Social Communication (Journalism). Federal University of Rio de Janeiro. 2010.
Philosophy (unfinished). State University of Rio de Janeiro. 2005-2008.


Professional experience

Medialab/UFRJ. Associate Researcher. 2017 - 2022
School of Data Brazil. Program Manager. 2018 - 2022
WITNESS. Consultant. 2018-2019
UNESCO. Consultant. 2016-2018
Agência Pública. Editor and reporter. 2014-2018
Instituto Mais Democracia. Content Manager. 2012-2013.
Government of the State of Rio de Janeiro - Secretary of Culture. Digital Culture Manager. 2010-2012.

title: European DDJ
text:
ChatGPT and large language models have recently gained immense attention in the media. However, beyond the hype, there are still more questions than answers on how journalists can effectively use and report on these technologies. This workshop introduces concepts that lay at the heart of modern natural language processing (NLP) techniques, such as word vectorisation and transformer models, and then builds on those concepts to discuss the current journalistic coverage and practical applications in newsrooms.
The workshop is structured in two main parts. First, the objective is to arrive at an intuitive understanding of the underlying concepts and unpack the technical jargon that clouds the domain of large language models (LLMs). We will discuss how journalists currently cover critical emerging technologies, such as the risks in anthropomorphizing conversational models. This discussion will be informed by what language models can and cannot do, as well as where the field is headed.
Following the discussion, a set of low-code NLP tools will be showcased. These tools are open-source, and many journalists can potentially integrate them into their daily workflow for tasks like text classification or entity recognition. One of these tools, Whisper, an audio transcription tool developed by OpenAI, will be explored in some depth through a hands-on demo. Ultimately, the purpose of the workshop is to explore the utility and limits of NLP, introduce key concepts that are required for greater critical reportage on the topic, and evaluate the potential and risks associated with the deployment of practical tools in newsrooms.
You can access the slides and notebook on the Github repository: https://github.com/belisards/nlp_intro

title: Forced Displacement Microdata
text:
The Forced Displacement Microdata dashboard lists publicly available datasets that have a representative sample of refugees or internally displaced people (IDP); by country, year, theme, data producer among others. I customized the Shiny dashboard based on the Eviatlas package.

Dashboard: https://rstudio.unhcr.org/FDMAtlas/
Code: https://github.com/belisards/FDPDataAtlas
Paper: Data Gaps in Microdata in the Context of Forced Displacement by Takaaki Masaki and B. Madson.
